claimText: Rare Earth Elements (REEs) comprise a group of 17 lustrous, silvery-white, soft heavy metals in the periodic table, including Cerium, Yttrium, and Europium.
evidenceExcerpt: REEs are a set of 17 lustrous, silvery-white, soft heavy metals in periodic table, e.g., Cerium (Ce), Yttrium (Y), Europium (Eu), etc.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the composition and examples of REEs.
distractorHooks: Stating REEs are non-metals or radioactive elements
Changing the number of elements from 17 to another number
Listing non-REEs like Uranium or Thorium as examples

claimText: Rare Earth Elements are difficult to extract because they do not occur in concentrated forms, despite being found abundantly in nature.
evidenceExcerpt: Though found abundantly, however, they are difficult to extract as they are not found in concentrated form.
examUse: Assertion-Reason or statement-based question on the availability and extraction challenges of REEs.
distractorHooks: Stating REEs are extremely rare in the Earth's crust
Claiming REEs are easy to extract because they are found in highly concentrated ores

claimText: China has restricted the export of Rare Earth Elements (REEs) in retaliation to US tariffs.
evidenceExcerpt: In retaliation to US tariffs, China restricts exports of Rare Earth Elements (REEs).
examUse: Current affairs statement regarding global supply chain disruptions of critical minerals.
distractorHooks: Stating the US restricted REE exports to China
Claiming India is the country restricting REE exports
